mdContact.rpOrgName: Bureau of Land Management (BLM), Oregon State Office
mdContact.displayName: Name: Scott Kiefer Organization: Bureau of Land Management (BLM), Oregon State Office Position: GIS Specialist Role: Unknown

Name: Scott Kiefer Organization: Bureau of Land Management (BLM), Oregon State Office Position: GS0301 - Geographic Information System Specialist Role: Unknown

distorOrdPrc.ordInstr: Data are only available through the Oregon/Washington State office of the Bureau of Land Management.

citRespParty.rpIndName: U.S. Department of the Interior, Bureau of Land Management (BLM)

idCitation.resTitle: BLM OR Oregon and Washington Highways Line

dataIdInfo.idAbs: highways_arc: This file includes all state owned or maintained highways, connections, frontage roads, temporary traveled routes and located lines in the states of Oregon and Washington.
dataIdInfo.idPurp: This data provides a detailed view of the state highway system based on the Linear Referencing system (LRS) used by both the Oregon Department of Transportation and the Washington State Department of Transportation. 1:24k resolution.
dataIdInfo.idCredit: Geographic Information Services Unit, Oregon Department of Transportation and the Cartography/GIS Section of the Washington State Department of Transportation, Bureau of Land Management (BLM), Oregon State Office

LegConsts.useLimit: The BLM assumes no responsibility for errors or omissions. No warranty is made by the BLM as to the accuracy, reliability, or completeness of these data for individual use or aggregate use with other data; nor shall the act of distribution to contractors, partners, or beyond, constitute any such warranty for individual or aggregate data use with other data. Although these data have been processed successfully on computers of BLM, no warranty, expressed or implied, is made by BLM regarding the use of these data on any other system, or for general or scientific purposes, nor does the fact of distribution constitute or imply any such warranty. In no event shall the BLM have any liability whatsoever for payment of any consequential, incidental, indirect, special, or tort damages of any kind, including, but not limited to, any loss of profits arising out of the use or reliance on the geographic data or arising out of the delivery, installation, operation, or support by BLM.
LegConsts.othConsts: None, these data are considered public domain.

Consts.useLimit: These data are provided by Bureau of Land Management (BLM) 'as is' and might contain errors or omissions. The User assumes the entire risk associated with its use of these data and bears all responsibility in determining whether these data are fit for the User's intended use. The information contained in these data is dynamic and may change over time. The data are not better than the sources from which they were derived, and both scale and accuracy may vary across the data set. These data might not have the accuracy, resolution, completeness, timeliness, or other characteristics appropriate for applications that potential users of the data may contemplate. The User is encouraged to carefully consider the content of the metadata file associated with these data. These data are neither legal documents nor land surveys, and must not be used as such. Official records may be referenced at most BLM offices. Please report any errors in the data to the BLM office from which it was obtained. The BLM should be cited as the data source in any products derived from these data. Any Users wishing to modify the data should describe the types of modifications they have performed. The User should not misrepresent the data, nor imply that changes made were approved or endorsed by BLM. This data may be updated by the BLM without notification.

dataIdInfo.suppInfo: Dataset locally known as highways_arc This data is intended for use at scales of 1:24,000 or smaller.

Consts.useLimit: These data are provided by Bureau of Land Management (BLM) 'as is' and might contain errors or omissions. The User assumes the entire risk associated with its use of these data and bears all responsibility in determining whether these data are fit for the User's intended use. The information contained in these data is dynamic and may change over time. The data are not better than the sources from which they were derived, and both scale and accuracy may vary across the data set. These data might not have the accuracy, resolution, completeness, timeliness, or other characteristics appropriate for applications that potential users of the data may contemplate. The User is encouraged to carefully consider the content of the metadata file associated with these data. These data are neither legal documents nor land surveys, and must not be used as such. Official records may be referenced at most BLM offices. Please report any errors in the data to the BLM office from which it was obtained. The BLM should be cited as the data source in any products derived from these data. Any Users wishing to modify the data should describe the types of modifications they have performed. The User should not misrepresent the data, nor imply that changes made were approved or endorsed by BLM. This data may be updated by the BLM without notification.

LegConsts.othConsts: None, these data are considered public domain.

report.measDesc: Where appropriate, line segment ends have been snapped together to eliminate gaps at interchanges, intersections and along contiguous sections of roadway.
This theme contains all highways and related access features (interchange ramps, frontage roads, etc.) for the states of Oregon and Washington.

In Oregon, the Highway segment id, lrs, roadway id, and roadway type were obtained from the Oregon Department of Transportation's Hwy_seg shapefile. Interstate sign, US sign 1, US sign 2, ST sign 1, ST sign 2, and highway name were obtained from ODOT'S routes_xref_2005 shapefile. In Washington, Interstate sign, US sign 1, US sign 2, ST sign 1, ST sign 2 were populated from WSDOT's SR24kLRSLinesSPS shapefile, while roadway id and roadway type were populated by the Bureau of Land Management Oregon State Office. .Highway local name and highway class were created by the Bureau of Land Management Oregon State Office. OBJECTID, Shape and Shape_Length were generated from ArcGIS software. In Washington, State Route numbers were obtained from the 2005B State Highway Log.

Oregons highway data has been determined to meet or exceed NSDI map accuracy standards for 1:24,000 scale maps. Accuracy was determined by the combined use of USGS digital ortho photo quads and the NGS benchmarks to apply an accuracy statement to the highway linework via verification of known and visible NGS benchmarks on the orthoquads. The Washington data's positional accuracy has not been determined.
No vertical positional accuracy has been determined

prcStep.stepDesc: The shapefiles were imported to a personal geodatabase and reprojected.
Removed overlapping highways from dataset.

09-11-08: Populated the DSG_Name field with attribute from Burns district and Roseburg district back country byways layers they gave the state office. Also populated DSG_Name where it overlays with Back_Country_Byways layer in SDE. Our goal is to remove that layer from SDE once we can add a code to query back country byways, scenic routes, etc.
The highway sign and name attributes from routes_xref_2005 were spatially joined to the highway segments.
Manual corrections and verification of attribute transfer were made where necessary.
Highway class (hwy_class) attribute field added. Values populated based on highway sign attributes.

citRespParty.rpOrgName: Cartography/GIS Section, Washington State Department of Transportation (WSDOT)

srcCitatn.resTitle: 1:24,000 7.5 minute United States Geologic Survey Quadrangles

citRespParty.rpOrgName: United States Geologic Survey Denver, Colorado 80225 or Reston, Virginia 22092
Geographic Information Services Unit, Oregon Department of Transportation (ODOT)
Geographic Information Services Unit, Oregon Department of Transportation (ODOT)

prcStep.stepDesc: The Hwy_SEG and routes_xref_2005 shapefiles were obtained via ftp from ODOT.
Copied interstate, US, and state highways from the OR/WA BLM GTRN layer to fill gaps in the ODOT highway Layer
Attributed frontage and connector roads in Jackson and Josephine counties. The hwylocname and hwy_class fields were populated based on John McGlothlin's local data.

stepProc.rpOrgName: Northrop Grumman - BLM Oregon State Office

prcStep.stepDesc: Added highways from WA DOT source for the state of Washington. Coded RDWY_ID with new values 6 and 7 for cartographic purposes.

stepProc.rpOrgName: Northrop Grumman - BLM OR/WA State Office

prcStep.stepDesc: Corrected US Highway 30 attribute information for 64 segments near The Dalles. Highway 30 was originally incorrectly attributed as a State Highway. Both HWY_CLASS and US_SIGN_1 fields were populated with the correct information for these segments.

stepProc.rpOrgName: Northrop Grumman - BLM OR/WA State Office

prcStep.stepDesc: Fixed topology errors using these rules (must not overlap, must not self-overlap, must be single part). Worked with Gavin Hoban in Prineville also for questions about rdwy_typ overlap discrepencies.

stepProc.rpOrgName: Northrop Grumman - BLM OR/WA State Office

prcStep.stepDesc: Placed Succor Creek Road (from Oregon T22S-R46E Sec 28 to Oregon T27S-R46E Sec 11, connecting Oregon highway 201 to US 95) in BLM's editable GTRN dataset on advice of Vale district office GIS staff indicating that this is a dirt road.
Added portion of HWY 95 in Nothern Nevada as requested by Vale District Office
Updated Salem Highways - removed frontage, overpasses/underpasses from Highways and added them to GTRN. Updated connections and secondary roads to maintain connectivity. Bruce Ahrendt reviewed.
Updated Roseburg Highways - removed frontage, overpasses/underpasses from Highways and added them to GTRN. Updated connections and secondary roads to maintain connectivity. Gary Passow reviewed.
Updated Coos Bay Highways - removed frontage, overpasses/underpasses from Highways and added them to GTRN. Updated connections and secondary roads to maintain connectivity. Alan Ward reviewed.
Updated Burns Highways - removed 2 frontage roads and a connection - approved by Pam Keller
Updated Vale Highways - removed frontage, overpasses/underpasses from Highways and added them to GTRN. Updated connections and secondary roads to maintain connectivity. Brent Grasty reviewed.
Updated Eugene Highways - removed frontage, overpasses/underpasses from Highways and added them to GTRN. Updated connections and secondary roads to maintain connectivity. Jay Ruegger reviewed.
Updated Deschutes county highways within urban growth boundary areas Removed frontage roads, overpasses/underpasses from Highways and added them to GTRN.
Updated Prineville Highways - removed frontage, overpasses/underpasses from Highways and added them to GTRN. Updated connections and secondary roads to maintain connectivity. Greg Daniels ok'd.
Added a small segement S of KFalls to connect hwy 161 together for Mike Limb.
Removed Highway 227 in Douglas County and added as County Route 1 in GTRN.
Changed highway names from all-uppercase to mixed case. Correct arcs that were missing designation of Snake River-Mormon BCB. Standardized blanks in several fields to null.

stepProc.rpOrgName: U.S. Bureau of Land Management, OR/WA State Office

prcStep.stepDesc: Per Greg Daniels (district coordinator), I update a segment of road to the East of the Redmond Airport.
Per Thomas Jackson, edited a piece of W 11th Ave in Eugene.
Spatially updated HWY 410 and Alt 410 in WA due to landslide.
Updated intersection on WA HWY 82 near Yakima.
Split highway 42 at T30S, R9W, Sec. 11 per Shelley Moore and Mike Hubbard (Roseburg)
Updated Ridgefield WA entrance/exit.
Updated Longview WA entrance.
Per Gavin Hoban/Prineville GIS Coordinator, updated highways in Prineville to reflect Scenic Bikeways. Also, for Shelley Moore, removed a small zigzag at T22S-R05W section 21.

Per Gavin Hoban/Prineville - updated attributes in highways to include special designated area and name for Painted Hills Scenic Bikeway and updated section of Sutton Mountain Back Country Byway (special designated area and name).

enttyp.enttypd: A segment of highway between two intersecting locations of other highway segments
enttyp.enttypds: Geographic Information Services Unit, Oregon Department of Transportation.

attr.attrdef: A unique identifier assigned to the roadway.
attr.attrdefs: Oregon Transportation Management System (OTMS) GIS Base Layer Development Project (GIS01)

attr.attrdef: This is a representation of elements contained in the State of Oregon’s database used to identify a highway, connection, or frontage road. The first nine characters are the inventory route numbers and the last three characters are the sub-route numbers.
attr.attrdefs: Oregon Transportation Management System (OTMS) GIS Base Layer Development Project (GIS01)

attrdomv.udom: The twelve digit alphanumeric string is broken down into its components as: 1: Place holder 0 (zero) 2-4: Hwy Number 001 to 499 5-6: Hwy Suffix 00 (zero zero), AA-ZZ (alphabetic characters) 7: Roadway Identifier (1-5) 8: Mileage Type (Z,T,Y) 9: Overlap Mileage Code (0-9) 10: Jurisdiction Code (S) 11-12: Two Place holders 00 (zero zero)

attr.attrdef: A unique identifier assigned to the roadway designating a spur, frontage road, connection, or regular roadway.
attr.attrdefs: Oregon Transportation Management System (OTMS) GIS Base Layer Development Project (GIS01)

edom.edomvds: Geographic Information Services Unit (GISU) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation
Geographic Information Services Unit (GISU) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation
Geographic Information Services Unit (GISU) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation
Geographic Information Services Unit (GISU) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation

attr.attrdef: The number assigned to a roadway where more than one roadbed exists for the highway.
attr.attrdefs: Oregon Transportation Management System (OTMS) GIS Base Layer Development Project (GIS01)

edom.edomvd: 1 - The primary roadway. Direction of travel is in the direction of increasing mile points.
edom.edomvds: Integrated Transportation Information System (ITIS) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation

edom.edomvd: 2 – The secondary roadway. Direction of travel is in the direction of decreasing mile points.
edom.edomvds: Integrated Transportation Information System (ITIS) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation

edom.edomvd: 3 – A split roadway with the direction of travel in the direction of increasing mile points.
edom.edomvds: Integrated Transportation Information System (ITIS) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation

edom.edomvd: 4 – A split roadway with the direction of travel in the direction of decreasing mile points
edom.edomvds: Integrated Transportation Information System (ITIS) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation
Integrated Transportation Information System (ITIS) - Oregon Dept. of Transportation

edom.edomvd: 6 – Used to differentiate split highways for display at small scales (WA only).
edom.edomvds: Bureau of Land Management - Oregon State Office

edom.edomvd: 7 – Used to differentiate split highways for display at small scales (WA only).
edom.edomvds: Bureau of Land Management - Oregon State Office

attr.attrdef: The official name of a specially designated route.

attr.attrdef: Special designation routes are routes that have been identified through formal national, state, or agency designation processes to have a level of scenic, natural, cultural, recreational, or archeological importance beyond a traffic thoroughfare. More than one special designation may apply to a single route segment. If this is the case, the value that is highest in the hierarchy is assigned.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

overview.eaover: Attributes are provided that give basic information about source, type, and accuracy of the line data. More information may be found in the data standard if available: https://www.blm.gov/about/data/oregon-data-management
